safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_tg + zero_live: $70-74 discount buy zone = weekly breaker + breakout origin + point-of-control (high-volume node). Mid-zone 72; price has fallen into this area (2026-06-18 chart ~74.46). Zero (2026-06-22) says 'appealing even from here'.

targets.basis: zero_live TP1 = gap fill; matches the 'GAP' label ~83-84 on the 2026-06-18 daily chart
zero_live TP2 = value-at-high (~89); VAH zone ~88-90 on charts and monthly main target 0.886 (~88-90)
POC on 2026-06-18 daily chart (~96) — stretch target if momentum continues (estimated as next structural level above 89)

safest_plan.stop_basis: Daily close below 70 (weekly breaker / discount low). Price may wick down to fill the monthly gap ~66-68 first, but Zero does not expect lower; a daily close under 70 breaks the setup.

stance_by_source.summary: Inside the weekly breaker with a possible re-accumulation; long still valid, buy ~70 (stop = monthly gap just below), first target the gap ~83 then ~89 — appealing even at current price.

Daily chart with price ~74.46 sitting right on the discount/breakout-origin zone; targets above are the gap ~83-84, then point-of-control ~96 and value-area-high ~100-104; monthly gap ~66-68 below as the floor.
Earlier daily chart with price ~91; same map — supply ~104-108 above, value-area-high ~100, demand/discount ~76-80 below where price has since fallen into.
Monthly macro view: accumulate in the discounted 50s-70s, partial target ~70, value-area-high ~80, main target ~88-90, with an extended move toward ~110 the longer-run scenario.

Buy $70-74 (discount + weekly breaker + point-of-control); invalidated on a close below $70, may wick to fill the monthly gap first. Flags this as 2x-leverage CFD (non-spot) — the halal version is the oil-company share COP.

conflicts: No directional disagreement — every source is bullish and points to the same 70-74 buy area and ~83 / ~89 targets, so this is genuinely aligned rather than contested.

Instrument caveat (not a directional conflict): Zero trades oil here as a leveraged CFD, which is not spot-owned and is the haram route; his clean/halal expression of this same view is buying the oil-company equity COP instead of the oil contract.
The June 10 charts showed price at ~91 and the June 18 chart at ~74 — this is the price falling INTO the intended discount buy zone, i.e. confirmation of the plan, not a contradiction.
plain_english: Every source here is on the same side: this is a buy. Oil has dropped into the 70-74 area that Zero flagged as his preferred buy zone — a long-term discount level where buyers stepped in before — and he says he'd add even at today's roughly 74 price. The plan only fails if oil closes below 70 (it might briefly dip toward a price gap around 66-68 first, but a daily close under 70 means step out); the first target is the price gap near 83, then the 88-90 area. One important caveat: Zero is trading oil here as a leveraged contract, which isn't actually owning the asset and isn't the halal route — his clean version of this trade is buying the oil-company share COP instead. If you can only trade spot/halal, treat this as a directional read and use COP, not the oil contract; otherwise the buy zone is 70-74 with a stop on a daily close below 70.
